item.title: Hedge funds suffered worst losses since Covid in Iran war turmoil
item.description: Managers have struggled to navigate huge market swings sparked by conflict

item.title: Air Canada to launch arbitration test project to settle passenger disputes
item.description: Test project aims to avoid long waits for customers seeking compensation

item.title: We found every data centre in Canada
item.description: The post We found every data centre in Canada appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Toyota keeps hydrogen as a hedge
item.description: Retaining flexibility may prove more valuable than going all in too early on a single bet

item.title: CK Hutchison takes Maersk to arbitration over Panama port
item.description: Hong Kong conglomerate escalates legal campaign over its ejection from canal terminals

item.title: European bonds surge as traders trim bets on interest rate rises
item.description: Government debt in UK and Eurozone on course for strongest day since 2023

item.title: UK house prices fall in March as uncertainty over Middle East war weighs on demand
item.description: Halifax reports market slowdown as mortgage rates rise

item.title: Ads are coming to AI chats. Behold as the Internet is ruined all over again
item.description: Once the advertising seal is broken, the lucrative revenue spigot will be hard for other AI companies to resist

item.description: FTSE 100 group expects a drop in gas production because of hit to facilities in Qatar

item.description: Donald Trump says 10-point plan from Iran is ‘workable basis’ for negotiations

item.title: Aldermore Bank put up for sale after owner attacks UK car finance redress scheme
item.description: South Africa’s FirstRand says industry-wide £9.1bn programme is ‘deeply flawed’

item.description: UK National Cyber Security Centre warns vulnerable routers will let attackers steal passwords and log-in details

item.description: None compare to the OG sovereign default

item.description: Reliance Industries has made no announcement to the stock exchange about the project in Brownsville, Texas

item.title: The ECB is stirring memories of 2011 — not all of them good
item.description: When the central bank tightened policy in an oil shock it proved a costly error. This time might be different

item.title: Investors still back green guidebook as Middle East war upends energy markets, taxonomy council chair says
item.description: The goal of the document is to attract at least $115-billion in capital needed annually for Canada to meet its net-zero targets by 2050

item.title: Private equity’s secondaries magic
item.description: Last year investors ploughed a record $166bn into the funds backing ageing assets

item.title: Long Covid to cost OECD economies up to $135bn a year
item.description: New research predicts impact of lower productivity and workers quitting because of long-term illness after Covid-19

item.title: Freedom itself is at stake in Hungary
item.description: Sunday’s election will show if it’s possible to defeat Orbán’s illiberal democracy Martin Wolf

item.title: How can we protect my mother’s estate after she passes away?
item.description: Her new partner lives with her and she wants to grant him the right to remain living in the property Tax

item.title: Turkey’s gold sales deepen bullion slump
item.description: Central bank arranged $20bn of sales and swaps in March as it battled to support the lira

item.title: When persistence prevails
item.description: After 593 monthly editions, a legend of role-playing games magazines has finally called it a day

item.title: Eramet’s top shareholder weighs exit as €500mn capital raise looms
item.description: Duval family hires Lazard to advise on 37% stake in troubled mining company

item.title: Perplexity revenue jumps 50% in pivot from search to AI agents
item.description: San Francisco-based start-up surges from push into more complex and potentially more lucrative AI services

item.title: US carmakers accuse EU of blocking supersized pick-up trucks from roads
item.description: Brussels is proposing to tighten safety rules that apply to a small number of specialist imports

item.title: How private equity became hooked on second-hand deals
item.description: Elite private capital firms have piled into the secondaries market as they race to attract assets

item.title: Will the Iran war derail the energy transition?
item.description: Countries in Asia and Europe are turning to coal to power their economies but analysts argue solar will be a long-term winner

item.title: The hangover from Britain’s student housing boom
item.description: Concerns about affordability and community impact are mounting, even as purpose-built accommodation gains traction elsewhere

item.title: London Borough Yards development handed to lender amid struggle to find tenants
item.description: Scheme’s difficulties signal fallout from pandemic is still being felt in real estate sector

item.title: The south-east Asian country at the sharp end of the energy crisis
item.description: In the Philippines, surging fuel prices have forced a rethink of energy policy and even relations with China

item.description: Tehran says it will allow ‘safe passage’ through Strait of Hormuz during two-week ceasefire

item.description: Global markets have been rattled since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran at the end of February

item.description: Gary Ng has made no attempt to repay $27.2-million in debts after he was declared bankrupt in 2023

item.description: The country is rapidly moving up the global ranks as an exporter of knowledge-intensive products

item.description: Markets are calling time on many Donald-adjacent bets

item.description: Purchasing power isn’t simply about how many groceries you can buy. It’s also about how much wealth a person can accumulate

item.description: When asked how you are navigating today’s climate of lower yields, you delivered the goods

item.description: Top trade official says he wants to avoid renegotiating the ‘load-bearing pillars’ of the pact that work well

item.description: Carnival is paying price for not locking in its fuel exposure when compared to competitors

item.description: Soaring prices for fertiliser and fuel could cause a global food shock

item.title: Canadian economic activity shrank last month for first time since November, Ivey PMI shows
item.description: Seasonally adjusted index fell to 49.7, below the 50 threshold indicating a contraction

item.title: Samsung to discontinue messaging app in July, asks users to switch to Google Messages
item.description: Switching will give users access to Google’s latest AI features from Gemini

item.title: IMF warns of emerging markets’ exposure to ‘flighty’ hedge funds
item.description: Non-bank lenders rapidly reduce holdings of EM debt during shocks such as the Iran war, analysis suggests

item.title: BP chair faces re-election battle after board blocks climate resolution
item.description: Glass Lewis recommends voting against Albert Manifold at annual meeting, while L&G also plans to oppose him

item.title: Nine of 10 Vizsla Silver workers confirmed dead after January abductions in Mexico
item.description: Area where the Vancouver-based miner operates is now controlled by a faction of the Sinaloa cartel, Mexico’s Security Minister says

item.title: Nova Scotia companies nearly doubled their solar power capacity in 2025
item.description: Province’s private power utility is aiming to have 80% of its energy come from renewables by 2030

item.description: Iran has proposed a ‘workable’ 10-point peace plan that could help end the war, U.S. President says

item.description: Controlling the flow of fossil fuels and food equals power

item.description: Middle-power collaboration puts Canada in a delicate situation ahead of pending negotiations with the U.S. and Mexico

item.description: Beijing lobbying stepped up at UN meetings over summit for high seas

item.description: War is splitting the country in two

item.title: Majority of Canadians open to joining EU, new poll suggests
item.description: One in four respondents thought it would be a good idea to formally join the European Union, while 58% thought it worth considering further

item.title: U.S. Fed official says rate hike possible if inflation remains elevated
item.description: Rate increases would be sharp shift from late last year, when Federal Reserve cut key rate three times

item.title: Canada’s services economy shrinks for fifth straight month in March
item.description: Uncertainty stemming from Middle East war has hampered new business and raised operating expenses

item.title: Barrick Mining slows development of Pakistan project amid security concerns
item.description: Miner expects review could lead to significant increase in Reko Diq’s capital budget and timeline

item.title: Sales of used EVs surge in US as petrol prices pass $4 a gallon
item.description: Americans are buying second-hand electric vehicles even as the market for new vehicles slumps

item.title: An Economist’s Quest to Solve America’s Wage Problem
item.description: Arindrajit Dube argues that the answer is empowering workers and setting mandatory wage standards across industries.

item.title: Quebecor’s Freedom Mobile is enjoying a newfound status
item.description: The telecom carrier is taking advantage of a federal policy that gives it access to rivals’ networks and expires in 2030

item.title: Why doesn’t Ontario want its pension funds to manage its new investment agency?
item.description: Ontario’s taxpayers deserve answers on how the government intends to spend their money

item.description: The Second Narrows Rail Bridge plays a critical role in shipping at Canada’s biggest and busiest port

item.title: How to fight car thefts, fentanyl and lift our economy – all at once
item.description: Unlike in other countries, shipping manifest data is a black hole in Canada – the public does not know what goes in and out

item.title: Power-starved Cuba deepens reliance on Chinese solar tech
item.description: Imports of the technology have soared, offering some respite for country that now faces near-total oil blockade

item.title: World’s biggest battery maker takes ambitions to the high seas
item.description: China’s CATL wants to electrify global shipping fleets but hurdles remain to large-scale adoption
